sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient is a 50-year-old male who presented with a 5-day history of right knee pain following a water skiing accident.
He reported that he twisted his knee when landing from a jump and felt a stretching sensation, which was followed by swelling.
He denies hearing or feeling anything pop.
He was able to get out of the water with assistance, but experienced pain with squatting and bending.
This is his first knee injury.
Past medical history: The patient had his tonsils out.
He is currently taking lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension, which he has been taking every day and checking his blood pressure at home, reporting a reading of 125/80 most days.
He has been using over-the-counter aleve twice a day and applying cold packs to the knee for pain relief.
Review of systems: The patient denies numbness or tingling in the foot.
Current medications:
Lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure is 120/77 in the office, which is slightly elevated from his home readings.
The examination revealed ecchymosis, edema, and an effusion in the right knee, with pain to palpation of the right medial knee.
The patient had a decreased range of motion, negative varus and valgus test, negative lachman sign, palpable dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulse, and no lower extremity edema.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a medial collateral ligament strain in the right knee.
Problem list: Medial collateral ligament strain; Hypertension; Past: Tonsillectomy.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15mg once daily to reduce swelling and pain in the right knee.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the muscles around the knee.
I ordered an x-ray of the right knee to rule out any broken bones.
I will continue the patient on lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension.
I also ordered a lipid panel to ensure everything is okay from that standpoint.
The patient will receive a tetanus shot for health maintenance.
Follow-up: The patient will return for follow-up after the x-ray results and to reassess the knee.
